text:
I sent 10 cold DMs offering AI content writing last week.

Results:
→ 7 no reply
→ 2 "not interested"
→ 1 asked for samples

Not glamorous. But that's the actual starting point nobody shows you.

Day 2. Still going. #BuildInPublic

Tested Gamma.app this week for a client pitch deck.

Actual result: 80% usable in 4 minutes. Spent another 20 fixing the layout.

Not magic. But definitely faster than starting from scratch.

The tool isn't the business. The client is. #AITools

Faceless YouTube with AI voice sounds simple.

Here's what nobody mentions:
→ Script still takes 1-2 hours to get right
→ ElevenLabs free tier = 10k chars/month (1-2 videos)
→ YouTube takes 3-6 months to monetize

Worth it long-term. Just not fast. #HonestAI

The real Fiverr AI play isn't competing on price.

It's finding a service with 100+ orders and a 3.8-star average.

That's a fixable gap. Enter with better delivery, same price.

Took me 3 hours to research. Zero dollars spent. #SideHustle #AI

ElevenLabs voice quality in 2026 is genuinely hard to tell apart from human.

I ran an experiment: sent the same script in my own voice and ElevenLabs to 5 friends.

3 couldn't tell which was AI.

That's the actual state of the tool. Make of it what you will. #AITools

My formula for writing content that doesn't sound like everyone else:

1. Start with what actually happened (specific)
2. Add the number that surprised you
3. One sentence conclusion
4. Ask a real question

Not a formula. More like: be specific, then shut up. #ContentCreation

I asked ChatGPT to be my business strategist.

The advice was solid. Generic, but solid.

The actual value wasn't the strategy — it was having something to react to instead of starting from zero.

AI as a sparring partner beats AI as an oracle. #AI

Honest ranking of AI side hustles by actual difficulty (not YouTube difficulty):

Easy to start, hard to scale: logo design, blog writing
Hard to start, easier to scale: chatbot services, automation
Hard both ways: consulting, SaaS

Start easy. Don't stay there. #AI #SideHustle

Nobody needs to hustle harder.

The actual problem is most people are busy with low-leverage tasks.

AI doesn't give you more hours. It removes the parts of your work that shouldn't be done by a human anyway.

What have you automated this month? #AI

Week 1 update — building from $0 with AI:

→ 20+ posts published
→ Views: mostly single digits
→ Followers gained: 0
→ Money made: $0
→ Lessons learned: content alone doesn't work without distribution

Week 2 goal: stop broadcasting, start engaging. #BuildInPublic

If you had to pick one AI tool to learn in 2026, make it ChatGPT.

Not because it's the best at everything.

Because understanding how to give instructions to AI transfers to every other tool you'll ever use.

It's the skill under the skill. #AI

What week 1 of building in public actually taught me:

- Consistency without strategy is just noise
- Starting with one income stream, not five, is harder than it sounds
- AI amplifies your judgment — good or bad
- The system matters more than the tools

Week 2 tomorrow. #BuildInPublic

The $10K/month AI screenshot is almost always month 6-12, not month 1.

Month 1 looks like:
→ $0
→ A few followers
→ One method that sort of worked

I'm in month 1. Keeping it real.

#BuildInPublic #AI

Nobody talks about the prompting learning curve.

Week 1: outputs are generic
Week 3: you learn to give context
Week 6: you have a system

The tool doesn't change. Your instructions do.

Clear thinking → clear prompts → useful output. That's the actual skill. #AI #NoCode

Most viral AI tools on TikTok have one thing in common: great demo, mediocre daily use.

I've been testing them. The ones that survive day 3 are usually the boring ones:
→ ChatGPT
→ Notion AI
→ One automation tool

Hype fades. Utility stays. #AITools

2 hours a day is enough — if it's the right 2 hours.

Most people spend them consuming (YouTube, Twitter, newsletters).

The shift that matters: 30 min learning, 90 min making something.

Ratio changes everything. #AI #BuildInPublic

Starting an AI side hustle sustainably means ignoring the $5K month 1 crowd.

Real path: small → consistent → iterate → scale.

$100 recurring beats $500 once. Every time.

Playing the long game here. #BuildInPublic

TikTok Creator Rewards pays more for 1-min+ original content now.

AI voiceover + useful information + no face = lowest barrier to entry I've seen.

Not saying it's easy. But the setup cost is basically $0.

Worth testing in parallel. #AI #SideHustle

The faceless creator model works — but the timeline people sell is wrong.

"10K followers in 30 days" = 50 videos + luck + right niche.

More honest: 3-4 months of consistent posting before meaningful traction.

Plan for that and you won't quit early. #FacelessCreator

AI-generated UGC is a real category now. Brands are paying for volume.

The play: 3 hooks per product, test fast, see what converts.

Not glamorous. But it's a real service with real buyers. Filed for later when I have more leverage. #UGC #AI

The AI content system I'm testing:

1. Topic research (ChatGPT + Reddit)
2. Draft (Claude)
3. Images (Midjourney or free)
4. Post + track

Day 5. Still in the "does this even work" phase.

I'll report back with actual numbers. #BuildInPublic #AI

Everyone's selling the AI income dream. Almost nobody is showing the month-by-month reality.

I'm going to be the latter.

Weekly numbers. What worked. What didn't. No polish.

If that sounds useful, stick around. #BuildInPublic

Doba Pilot caught my attention — an AI that supposedly builds your Shopify store and picks products automatically.

Skeptical. The demo looks smooth. Real use always has friction.

Adding to the test list. Will report what actually happens. #AI #Dropshipping

ECHO-ME is trying to let creators automate their DMs in their own voice.

The idea is smart: brand deal detection + fan engagement + revenue conversion, 24/7.

The execution risk is sounding like a bot. That kills trust faster than silence. #CreatorEconomy #AI

MuleRun's pitch: a self-evolving AI that learns your workflows and runs in its own VM.

You can buy or sell specialized agents on their marketplace.

If that actually works, it flips the freelancer model. You don't sell time — you sell an agent. Interesting direction. #AIWorkforce

ASCN is a no-code AI agent builder with a marketplace of ready-made automations.

Lead gen, sales pipelines, content — prebuilt and deployable.

For non-technical founders, this is the kind of tool that actually removes the "I can't code" excuse. #NoCode #AI

2026 shift that's actually real: AI is moving from answering questions to running tasks.

A year ago: chatbots.
Now: agents that operate browsers, manage workflows, execute without you watching.

The gap between knowing this and acting on it is where opportunity lives. #AI2026

I made a free ebook walking through 10 AI income methods I'm actually testing — with realistic timelines, not YouTube numbers.

Called it AI Money Blueprint. No paywall.

Link in bio if you want it. #AI #SideHustle

Spent time comparing 48+ AI tools by income potential, difficulty, and whether they have affiliate programs.

Put it in a free spreadsheet. Not a list — an actual comparison with columns that matter.

Check my profile to download it. #AITools

Wrote 100 prompts specifically for making money — not generic prompts.

Covers: content creation, outreach, pricing your service, automating delivery.

Free. No email required. Link in bio. #ChatGPT #AI

Three things I put together for anyone starting AI side hustles from zero:

→ A blueprint (10 methods, real timelines)
→ A tool comparison sheet (48+ tools)
→ 100 prompts for actual use cases

All free. All in my profile. #BuildInPublic #AI

Most AI tool lists tell you what a tool does. Mine tells you which ones have income potential and how hard it actually is to get there.

48+ tools compared. Free download in my profile. #AITools

100 prompts for making money with AI — not "write me a poem" prompts.

Things like: writing a cold outreach that doesn't sound like a template, pricing your first offer, automating client onboarding.

Free. Link in bio. #ChatGPT #SideHustle

If you're starting AI side hustles from scratch, the blueprint I made covers the 10 methods I'm personally testing — with honest timelines.

Not aspirational. Just what the research and early results show.

Free. Link in bio. #AI #MakeMoneyOnline

Built a free resource hub for this journey:
→ AI Money Blueprint (ebook)
→ AI Tools Comparison 2026 (spreadsheet)
→ 100 Money-Making Prompts

Three things. Zero paywalls. All practical.

Check my profile. #AITools #BuildInPublic

The AI tools comparison I made answers one question: which tools are actually worth learning if income is your goal?

48+ tools. Sorted by use case, difficulty, and income angle.

Free. Link in bio. #AI #AITools

Reddit thread asked: "Is anyone actually making money from AI apps?"

Honest answers buried in comments:
→ Yes, but 6-12 months in
→ Service businesses (not passive)
→ One method, done consistently

The hype is in the headlines. The reality is in the replies. #AI #BuildInPublic

The fastest real AI income path I've found so far:

Find a business owner doing something repetitive.
Automate one step with AI.
Charge monthly to maintain it.

No audience needed. No course. No waiting for an algorithm.

Still testing this. Will share results. #AI

Everyone posts the wins. I'm going to post both.

Week 1: 0 followers gained, single-digit views, $0 earned.

That's the real starting point. If it improves, you'll see it here. If it doesn't, you'll see that too.

#BuildInPublic #AI

The accounts actually making money with AI aren't posting "comment MONEY for the list."

They're quiet about methods. Public about results.

Building a specific niche. One real offer. Fewer tools, more systems.

Trying to be that account. #AI #BuildInPublic
